tldr: Move only the geocoding slice of your Mapbox usage to OpenCage for cost and simplicity — Mapbox tiles, navigation, and SDKs have no OpenCage equivalent.
why_switch: Lower per-request cost for high-volume geocoding.
Plain REST API — easy to integrate and easy to leave.
OSM-aggregated data with clear licensing posture.
Decouples geocoding cost from Mapbox map session pricing.
what_to_export: Address lists currently sent to Mapbox geocoding
Mapbox tokens and where they appear
Usage and quota history from Mapbox dashboard
Logging tied to Mapbox geocoding requests
export_method: Mapbox does not warehouse your geocoded results — pull them from your own database. Use the Mapbox account dashboard for usage history to size an OpenCage plan. Consult Mapbox's official docs for current usage-export and audit options.
import_method: OpenCage is stateless and has no bulk import; just point your geocoding code at OpenCage's forward/reverse endpoints with your API key, and backfill historical addresses in batches respecting rate limits. Refer to OpenCage's official docs for current endpoint URLs, parameters, and quotas.

concept_mapping.note: Stays on Mapbox or another renderer.

gotchas: OpenCage replaces geocoding only — keep Mapbox (or another renderer) for tiles, styles, and navigation.
Move geocoding behind your backend; don't expose OpenCage keys client-side.
Response shape differs; update components/formatted/confidence handling.
Watch rate limits on lower OpenCage tiers during backfills.
Cache results aggressively to control cost.
Re-check attribution and OSM licensing requirements.

email.subject: Moving geocoding from Mapbox to OpenCage
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposal: move just our geocoding workload from Mapbox to OpenCage. Mapbox tiles, styles, and navigation stay where they are — this is a targeted change to cut geocoding cost and reduce client-side coupling.

Plan:
1. Inventory every forward/reverse geocoding call site.
2. Move calls server-side and swap to OpenCage.
3. Update response parsing and biasing params.
4. Spot-check accuracy across our priority markets.
5. Keep Mapbox for rendering until/unless we revisit that separately.

Estimated effort: 1-3 days.

Thanks
